game_name: Super Mario RPG: Legend of the Seven Stars

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Familiar Mario mechanics as foundation for RPG hybrid
Training battles with avoidable encounters establish systems
Amplifies move effects; mastery determines battle efficiency
Introduces party management and character-specific abilities
Support role management in longer battles
High-damage specialized attacks for strategic combat

element_introduction_order.first_appears: After Smithy Gang invasion narrative point
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Former antagonist with high offense; recontextualizes story
3D spatial navigation blended with traditional Mario jumping

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Combat encounters with item option in menu

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Seven-piece macrogame structure drives narrative progression
Mountains, forests, bodies of water, cloud cities, underground areas
Significant difficulty spike; action command mastery required

typical_relief_triggers: Party member recovery through healing abilities
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Traditional JRPG cadence with town/dungeon/boss cycle; macro tension driven by seven Star Piece collection arc; micro tension from visible enemies and action command timing windows

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Visible enemies allow player to choose engagement vs. avoidance; hidden paths in isometric 3D spaces (question blocks, destructible elements)
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Isometric perspective reveals hidden alcoves and layered platforming paths; Mario's traditional below-block interactions (punching from underneath) adapted for 3D view

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle onboarding with safe Mario platforming mechanics; avoidable encounters allow pacing control; Mushroom Kingdom serves as extended tutorial
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive party additions introduce tactical depth; enemy variety increases; Smithy Gang lieutenants present moderate-to-significant spikes
difficulty_curve.late_game: Seven worlds include reformed Monstro Town (narrative complexity), Yoshi's Yo'ster Isle (world-specific mechanics), Nimbus Land (cloud-themed challenges), and Bowser's Keep reconquest; final approach to Smithy confrontation
notable_spikes: Claymorton/Mack boss (Mushroom Kingdom Castle) - first major action command test
Smithy Gang lieutenant encounters - escalating difficulty
Multi-part dungeon sequences without intermediate save points
Smithy final boss - comprehensive mechanic mastery required

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard JRPG structure: safe town with NPCs/shops → field overworld navigation → dungeon area with visible enemies → boss encounter → Star Piece acquisition → narrative progression
Each world region tied to party member origin or thematic relevance; architecture and enemy types reflect region inhabitants
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Moleville (moles), Yo'ster Isle (Yoshi), Nimbus Land (cloud people)

notable_level_patterns.description: 3D-perspective navigation challenges requiring precise jumping and directional input; layered vertical exploration distinct from traditional side-scrolling Mario
Enemies placed in field as objects; player chooses avoidance routes or engagement; eliminates random encounters and grants pacing agency
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All overworld and dungeon exploration sequences

notable_level_patterns.description: Story beats separate party members; subsequent world areas function as retrieval sequences; narrative justifies dungeon revisit or new area exploration
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Post-Exor invasion: Mario, Peach, Bowser separated; reunion drives world exploration
